perturbation.original_text: Party B shall arrive at the network partners determined by Party A according to the time and route stipulated in this Agreement, and strictly comply with the start time and end time. Unless otherwise approved by Party A, in the event of parcel transfer due to Party B's vehicle delay, Party B shall pay liquidated damages to Party A at the standard rate of RMB500 per trip on the first working day, RMB1,000 per trip on the second working day and RMB2,000 per trip on the third working day (based on the time records on the parcel transfer documents) and such liquidated damages will be deducted from the current month's freight. In the special event of changes to route and time and severe weather (or other force majeure events), Party B shall be in touch with Party A in time and record such special event on the parcel transfer documents with Party A's approval.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B shall make reasonable efforts to arrive at the network partners determined by Party A according to the time and route stipulated in this Agreement, and comply with the start time and end time to the best of its ability. Unless otherwise approved by Party A, in the event of parcel transfer due to Party B's vehicle delay, Party B *may* pay liquidated damages to Party A at the standard rate of RMB500 per trip on the first working day, RMB1,000 per trip on the second working day and RMB2,000 per trip on the third working day (based on the time records on the parcel transfer documents) and such liquidated damages *may* be deducted from the current month's freight. In the special event of changes to route and time and severe weather (or other force majeure events), Party B shall be in touch with Party A in time and record such special event on the parcel transfer documents with Party A's approval.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'shall strictly comply' to 'comply with the start time and end time to the best of its ability' and 'shall pay' to 'may pay' weakens the obligation on Party B to adhere to the time stipulations and pay damages. This introduces uncertainty about whether Party B is legally bound to specific arrival times and damage payments. In another part of the document it mentions 'Party A has the right to terminate this Agreement if Party B has breached the above articles in this Agreement'. But due to this perturbation, Party A has no basis to terminate the agreement anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party A has the right to terminate this Agreement if Party B has breached the above articles in this Agreement. The termination of this Agreement shall not prejudice Party A's right to hold Party B responsible for breach of contract.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens Party B's obligation to adhere to the time stipulations and pay damages by changing 'shall strictly comply' to 'comply with the start time and end time to the best of its ability' and 'shall pay' to 'may pay'. This introduces uncertainty about whether Party B is legally bound to specific arrival times and damage payments. This directly contradicts the clause stating 'Party A has the right to terminate this Agreement if Party B has breached the above articles in this Agreement' because if the obligation is weakened to 'best of its ability', a breach is harder to determine and thus makes the termination clause unenforceable. Thus it is an ambiguity-based in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Party B shall purchase sufficient insurance for the transportation vehicles. The coverage of third-party liability insurance shall not be lower than RMB1 million. In addition to vehicle personnel insurance, Party B shall at least purchase injury insurance for two persons with coverage not lower than RMB500,000 per person. Party B shall bear any consequence arising out of the non-compliance of insurance purchase, and Party A shall have no liability.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B shall purchase insurance for the transportation vehicles. The coverage of third-party liability insurance *should* not be lower than RMB1 million. In addition to vehicle personnel insurance, Party B *is encouraged to* purchase injury insurance for two persons with coverage not lower than RMB500,000 per person. Party B shall bear any consequence arising out of the non-compliance of insurance purchase, and Party A shall have no liability.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not be lower than' to 'should not be lower than' and 'shall at least purchase' to 'is encouraged to purchase', the mandatory nature of the insurance requirements is reduced. While the last sentence says Party B shall bear any consequence arising out of the non-compliance of insurance purchase, with the new perturbed definition, the term 'non-compliance' is not as forceful and has ambiguity and Party B can claim that it complied.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party B shall purchase insurance for the transportation vehicles. The coverage of third-party liability insurance should not be lower than RMB1 million. In addition to vehicle personnel insurance, Party B is encouraged to purchase injury insurance for two persons with coverage not lower than RMB500,000 per person. Party B shall bear any consequence arising out of the non-compliance of insurance purchase, and Party A shall have no liability.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall' to 'should' and 'is encouraged to' weakens the mandatory nature of the insurance requirements. The contradicted text, identical to the changed text, now lacks the strong obligation originally present, creating ambiguity regarding 'non-compliance'. This creates a contradiction because while Party B is to bear the consequences of non-compliance, the definition of compliance is no longer strongly defined and is encouraged, thereby making the meaning of bearing consequences unclear, which can lead to uncertainty and disputes about the extent of Party B's obligations, thus aligning with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Party B shall obtain Party A's written consent in the case the early termination of the Agreement. Party B shall pay one-month freight as liquidated damages in case of termination of the Agreement without consent. Within the contract period, Party B shall not charge the freight difference if Party A rents same-level vehicles. Party B shall also compensate Party A's other losses.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B *should* obtain Party A's written consent in the case the early termination of the Agreement. Party B *may* pay one-month freight as liquidated damages in case of termination of the Agreement without consent. Within the contract period, Party B shall not charge the freight difference if Party A rents same-level vehicles. Party B shall also compensate Party A's other losses.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall obtain' to 'should obtain' and 'shall pay' to 'may pay', the mandatory nature of obtaining written consent and paying liquidated damages is reduced to an optional action. In another section, Party A has the right to terminate this Agreement if Party B has breached the above articles in this Agreement, but due to this perturbation, Party A doesn't have the right to terminate the agreement anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party A has the right to terminate this Agreement if Party B has breached the above articles in this Agreement. The termination of this Agreement shall not prejudice Party A's right to hold Party B responsible for breach of contract.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'shall obtain' and 'shall pay', creating a mandatory obligation for Party B to obtain consent and pay damages. The changed text uses 'should obtain' and 'may pay', making these actions optional. The contradicted text states Party A can terminate the agreement if Party B breaches the 'above articles.' Because the changed text makes the obligations optional, it's unclear if a failure to obtain consent or pay damages is now a breach that allows termination, creating a direct contradiction and making Party A's right to terminate for these reasons unenforceable. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
